skill.name: Competitive Landscape Analysis
skill.description: Comprehensive frameworks for analyzing competition, identifying differentiation opportunities, and developing winning market positioning strategies using proven methodologies like Porter's Five Forces and Blue Ocean Strategy.
skill.summary: Business strategy skill providing competitive analysis frameworks for market positioning and differentiation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides business strategy frameworks for competitive landscape analysis. All static findings (14 external_commands, 1 weak cryptographic algorithm, 1 system reconnaissance) are false positives. The detected patterns are markdown text, ASCII diagrams, and business methodology content - no executable code exists. The skill is safe for publication.

high_findings.title: External Commands Pattern False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged markdown text and ASCII diagrams as Ruby/shell backtick execution. The implementation-playbook.md contains only business methodology documentation with no executable code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection confirms the file contains only markdown text, ASCII diagrams for positioning maps, and business framework descriptions. No Ruby, shell commands, or executable code exists.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 223 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' - the line contains 'Better design/UX' which is business text, not code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 223 contains plain text describing UX differentiation, not cryptographic code.

high_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 347 as 'system reconnaissance' - this is a pricing comparison table, legitimate competitive analysis content.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 347 is part of a pricing comparison matrix with competitor pricing data - standard competitive analysis methodology, not reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: This skill helps users analyze their competitive environment using proven business frameworks. It provides structured methodologies like Porter's Five Forces and Blue Ocean Strategy to identify market positioning opportunities.
actual_capabilities: Apply Porter's Five Forces to assess industry attractiveness
Create competitive positioning maps to identify market gaps
Use Blue Ocean Strategy to find uncontested market space
Develop differentiation strategies across product, service, brand, and price
Build competitor profiles with strengths, weaknesses, and strategies
Establish competitive monitoring cadence for ongoing intelligence
limitations: Does not provide real-time competitor data or pricing
Cannot access external company databases or private information
Requires user to provide specific competitor and market information
Framework output depends on quality of user-provided inputs

use_cases.title: Startup Market Entry Analysis
use_cases.description: New startup founders use this skill to understand competitive dynamics before launching a product, identifying blue ocean opportunities and defensible market positions.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Competitive Review
use_cases.description: Business analysts conduct quarterly competitive reviews for established companies, mapping competitor positions and identifying strategic threats.

use_cases.title: Investment Due Diligence
use_cases.description: Investors evaluate market attractiveness and competitive intensity for investment decisions, assessing industry forces and competitive dynamics.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Competitive Analysis Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to analyze the competitive environment for [industry/market]. Apply Porter's Five Forces to assess industry attractiveness and identify key competitive forces.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick competitive landscape overview

prompt_templates.title: Positioning Map Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to create a positioning map for [industry]. Plot competitors on [dimension 1: price vs. features] and [dimension 2: simplicity vs. complexity]. Identify white space opportunities.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to visualize competitive positions

prompt_templates.title: Differentiation Strategy Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to help develop a differentiation strategy for [company/product]. Consider product, service, brand, and price differentiation. Identify sustainable competitive advantages.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to define unique market positioning

prompt_templates.title: Blue Ocean Strategy Workshop
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitive-landscape skill to apply the Four Actions Framework (Eliminate, Reduce, Raise, Create) for our [product/service]. Identify opportunities to create uncontested market space.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to find new market opportunities

output_examples.input: Analyze the SaaS project management market using Porter's Five Forces
output_examples.output:
Porter's Five Forces Analysis for SaaS Project Management:

1. Threat of New Entrants: HIGH - Low capital requirements, cloud infrastructure accessibility
2. Bargaining Power of Suppliers: LOW - Multiple cloud providers available
3. Bargaining Power of Buyers: HIGH - Many options, easy switching
4. Threat of Substitutes: MEDIUM - Spreadsheets, manual processes
5. Competitive Rivalry: VERY HIGH - 20+ established competitors

Overall: Low industry attractiveness due to high rivalry and buyer power. Differentiation and focus strategy recommended.

output_examples.input: Create a positioning map for the email marketing industry
output_examples.output:
Positioning Map: Email Marketing Platforms

                    High Features
                          ★ Klaviyo
                ★ Mailchimp  ● Enterprise
                          |
                          |      ● Us (mid-market focus)
                          |
    ● Simple    ★ SendGrid ★ Competitor D
                          |
                          ‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾‾
                    Low Price → High Price

White Space Identified: Premium self-serve segment (high features, mid price)

best_practices: Gather specific competitor data before analysis to ensure accurate framework application
Use multiple frameworks (Porter's, Blue Ocean, positioning maps) for comprehensive view
Validate findings with real customer interviews and market research
anti_patterns: Applying frameworks without understanding your specific market context
Using generic competitor lists instead of direct competitors
Ignoring substitute products and alternative solutions

faq.question: What is Porter's Five Forces?
faq.answer: Porter's Five Forces is a strategic framework that analyzes five key forces affecting industry attractiveness: threat of new entrants, bargaining power of suppliers, bargaining power of buyers, threat of substitutes, and competitive rivalry.

faq.question: How is this skill different from a market research tool?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured frameworks and methodologies for analysis. You provide the competitor and market data; the skill applies proven strategic frameworks to help you organize and interpret that information.

faq.question: Can this skill find competitors for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires you to identify and provide competitor information. It applies analysis frameworks to the data you provide rather than gathering data itself.

faq.question: What is Blue Ocean Strategy?
faq.answer: Blue Ocean Strategy is a methodology for creating uncontested market space by eliminating, reducing, raising, and creating factors in your industry. It helps find market opportunities where competition is irrelevant.

faq.question: How often should I update my competitive analysis?
faq.answer: Conduct deep competitive reviews quarterly, with weekly monitoring of competitor news and product changes. Reassess major strategic assumptions annually.

faq.question: Does this work for B2B and B2C markets?
faq.answer: Yes, the frameworks apply to both B2B and B2C markets. You may need to adjust dimensions for positioning maps and consider different buyer behavior patterns.
